mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an impending economic contraction that erodes corporate revenue expectations, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the subsequent 5 trading days as risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between consumer credit expansion and housing starts indicates a structural shift in aggregate demand composition. When credit growth outpaces housing activity, equity valuations adjust downward over a 60-day horizon as capital expenditure cycles stall.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal direction flips consistently across market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while SPX forward returns remain negative for more than 4 consecutive quarters, invalidating the supply-demand profit channel.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate signals strong aggregate demand, which translates directly into higher corporate earnings per share and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth remains above trend for two consecutive periods while the S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, the cost of capital exceeds the historical average return on equity, forcing equity multiples to contract and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive for more than 60% of observations after the rate threshold is breached, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion. This dual signal reduces recession probabilities and compresses equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the Consumer Price Index above 3.0 percent triggers inflation expectations that erode real purchasing power and force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive forward returns in more than 65% of instances following the inflation threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.
